title: Uplifting German Marzipan Tagine
description: A peppery, fluffy tagine that blends German marzipan sweetness with aromatic spices to soothe hangovers and delight the palate.
instructions: Peel and cube sweet potato, carrot, courgette, and red bell pepper into bite‑sized pieces.
Heat olive oil in a tagine pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add cumin and turmeric to the oil and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the chopped vegetables and sauté for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the minced ginger, and cook for another minute.
Pour in the vegetable stock, lemon juice, and salt, then bring to a gentle simmer.
Add marzipan paste and stir until fully dissolved and the sauce thickens slightly.
Reduce heat to low, cover the tagine, and let it simmer for 25 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary, adding a splash more salt or lemon if desired.
Remove from heat, fluff the mixture with a fork, and transfer to a serving dish.
Serve hot straight from the tagine for a comforting, hangover‑cure meal.
